markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games has locked GTA VI for a November 19, 2026 console launch on PS5 and Xbox Series X/S after multiple delays from an original 2025 window, giving traders a defined window until then for any “before” resolution. The latest official statement emphasized extra polishing time, with Take-Two’s May earnings call the next likely checkpoint for updates on pre-orders or further shifts. No new trailer or major gameplay footage has dropped in over a year, keeping hype focused on when Rockstar will next communicate. Broader industry context includes typical Rockstar marketing ramps in the final 12–18 months and the game’s status as the biggest entertainment event on the horizon, making any high-profile album drop, geopolitical development, or tech milestone in the interim a potential catalyst for market movement.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Harvey Weinstein’s advanced age of 74, documented health decline including wheelchair use, and more than six years already served have anchored trader consensus around no additional prison time at 76% implied probability. The May 15, 2026, mistrial on the remaining New York rape charge—following the 2024 overturn of his original conviction and a mixed 2025 retrial verdict—leaves key counts unresolved while his separate 16-year California sentence faces appeals. Ongoing plea discussions, poor conditions at Rikers, and historical patterns of leniency for elderly defendants with medical issues further support the market view that any future penalties would likely run concurrent or be offset by time credits, with sentencing timelines remaining uncertain amid continued litigation.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights have reached the 2026 Stanley Cup Final as the last two teams standing, driving trader consensus reflected in the 58.5% and 41.4% implied probabilities. Carolina advanced with a dominant 4-1 Eastern Conference Final series win over Montreal, including a 6-1 clincher and an overall 12-1 postseason mark fueled by strong regular-season form topping the East with 113 points. Vegas earned its spot by sweeping Colorado in the Western Conference Final after earlier series victories, capitalizing on momentum and defensive play. These recent playoff results and head-to-head positioning in the best-of-seven series shape the current market sentiment ahead of Game 1.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping’s entrenched control over the Chinese Communist Party and Central Military Commission, reinforced by extensive anti-corruption purges that have removed senior military and party figures without evident elite backlash, underpins the 92.7% trader consensus favoring “No.” Ongoing leadership transitions ahead of the 21st Party Congress in late 2027 center on loyalty consolidation rather than succession, with Xi actively directing the 15th Five-Year Plan rollout, economic stability measures, and high-level diplomacy in 2026. No public health issues, institutional challenges, or designated heir have emerged to suggest removal before year-end 2026. Rare scenarios such as sudden elite revolt or unforeseen health events could still shift probabilities, though current indicators point to continuity through the resolution window.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom holds the top position in the fragmented 2028 Democratic presidential nomination field through his high national visibility as California governor, frequent media engagements, and positioning as a leading critic of the current administration, though recent national polls show variation with Pete Buttigieg or others sometimes ahead. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Kamala Harris follow at lower shares, drawing support from progressive voters and prior national exposure respectively, while governors and senators like Josh Shapiro and Jon Ossoff trail amid early donor outreach and trips to key Southern states. No candidates have formally declared, leaving outcomes dependent on post-2026 midterm polling trends, endorsements, fundraising, and primary dynamics before any consolidation occurs.
